commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redirect unscoped WP URLs back to scoped WP URLs in service worker (#3370)

## Summary
- When the service worker intercepts a request from a scoped page
(identified via the Referer header) but the request URL itself is
unscoped, redirect it to the scoped version of the URL instead of
directly handling it as a PHP request. This prevents scope loss when
navigating around a scoped WordPress instance.
- Skip intercepting cross-origin requests initiated from scoped pages,
letting them pass through to the network instead.

## Context

Previously, when a scoped WordPress page linked to an unscoped URL (e.g.
a WordPress-generated link without the scope prefix), the service worker
would directly handle it via `handleScopedRequest()`. This worked for
that single request, but the resulting page would be loaded at an
unscoped URL. Navigating from that unscoped page to another unscoped URL
resulted in losing the scope entirely, and the service worker would no
longer be able to match requests to the correct WordPress instance.

By redirecting to the scoped URL instead, the browser's address bar and
subsequent navigations all stay within the scope.

## Test plan
- [ ] Load a scoped WordPress Playground instance
- [ ] Navigate to a WordPress link that doesn't include the scope prefix
- [ ] Verify the browser redirects to the scoped version of the URL
- [ ] Verify subsequent navigation continues to work within the scope
- [ ] Verify cross-origin requests from scoped pages are not intercepted

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: wordpress-playground
repository.description: Run WordPress in the browser via WebAssembly PHP

commit.message:
refactor: improve error handling, types, and documentation

Preserve original error context in ScheduleFetchError, tighten formatter
map types with enum unions for compile-time exhaustiveness, and document
why LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams requires a transaction client.

Changes:
- Add cause option to ScheduleFetchError constructor and pass original error
- Add JSDoc to LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams explaining FOR UPDATE requirement
- Type SKILL_LEVEL_LABELS and LEARNING_STYLE_LABELS with enum Record types
- Narrow formatSkillLevel/formatLearningStyle parameters to enum unions
- Remove formatter tests for invalid string inputs (now compile errors)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): improve rate limit headers and error metadata

getRateLimitHeaders included Retry-After on every response, violating
RFC 6585 which reserves it for 429/503. RateLimitError only carried
retryAfter but the class supports limit/remaining/reset fields.

Changes:
- Remove Retry-After from getRateLimitHeaders (X-RateLimit-* only)
- Include limit, remaining, and reset in RateLimitError on rejection
- Update tests to reflect Retry-After removal from success headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): centralize stream cleanup in useRetryGeneration

Early returns on terminal SSE events (complete, error, cancelled) did
not cancel the reader or abort the controller, causing resource leaks.
Wrapping the reader loop in try/finally ensures cleanup on every exit
path.

Changes:
- Wrap reader loop and buffer-flush in inner try/finally block
- Call reader.cancel() and controller.abort() in finally clause

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): bind OAuth state token redemption to provider

The state redemption was not bound to the issuing provider, allowing a
token issued for one provider to be redeemed against another. consume()
now requires and verifies a provider parameter, preventing cross-provider
token replay attacks.

Changes:
- Add provider parameter to OAuthStateStore.consume interface
- Add eq(provider) to the WHERE clause in consume implementation
- Update validateOAuthStateToken to require and forward provider
- Pass google_calendar provider in Google OAuth callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(logging): align correlation ID header with proxy

The proxy sets x-correlation-id on every request, but REQUEST_ID_HEADER
in request-context.ts was x-request-id. createRequestContext therefore
never found the correlation ID the proxy set, generating a fresh UUID
and breaking request correlation across the stack.

Changes:
- Change REQUEST_ID_HEADER from x-request-id to x-correlation-id
- attachRequestIdHeader automatically picks up the new value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update APS badge instruction to include APS_BADGE constant sync

The instruction previously only mentioned updating README.md but not
the APS_BADGE constant in AGENTS.md, which would cause drift.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix any_weighted panic on empty iterator in Random mode (#79)

* Initial plan

* Add tests confirming empty-input regression in any_weighted

Co-authored-by: Magicolo <5423040+Magicolo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix any_weighted panic on empty iterator in Random mode

The refactor in 92ad30b removed the empty-input guard when switching
from slice-based to iterator-based API. Add a guard after computing
total weight to return None for empty iterators instead of panicking.

Co-authored-by: Magicolo <5423040+Magicolo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Return None when weighted selection fails

Remove early total > 0.0 guard and replace unreachable!() with a None
return so weighted selection yields None (e.g. zero total weight)
instead of
panicking. Keeps debug assertions for finite totals.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Magicolo <5423040+Magicolo@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: apply code quality improvements across helpers and tests

Batch of small improvements from code review: improve JSDoc, add return
types, export interfaces for testability, make non-transactional DB
helpers more ergonomic, and add test isolation.

Changes:
- Expand readHeader JSDoc with param/return docs and HeaderSource behavior
- Add explicit SQL | undefined return type to userAttemptsSincePredicate
- Make dbClient optional in OwnedPlanQueryParams (defaults to getDb())
- Add LockedOwnedPlanQueryParams with required dbClient for lockOwnedPlanById
- Export OAuthStateStore interface for typed test doubles
- Add beforeEach resetDbForIntegrationTestFile in monitoring-queries test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: validate weekNumber in getWeekBoundaries

Add upfront validation that weekNumber is an integer >= 1, throwing a
RangeError for zero, negative, or non-integer values that would produce
shifted or partial week boundaries.

Changes:
- Add Number.isInteger and >= 1 check with RangeError
- Add test cases for weekNumber 0, -1, and 1.5

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent double-firing and flush decoder in useRetryGeneration

Add a ref-based synchronous guard to prevent concurrent retry POST
requests from rapid clicks. Flush the TextDecoder after the read loop
to process any remaining buffered bytes before falling through to the
error state.

Changes:
- Add retryInFlightRef to block concurrent in-flight requests
- Reset ref in finally block, independent of cooldown timer
- Flush decoder with stream:false after read loop exits
- Process remaining buffer lines for terminal SSE events

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): wrap schedule generation errors in ScheduleFetchError

Wrap distributeTasksToSessions in try-catch to maintain a consistent API
error contract. Add SCHEDULE_GENERATION_FAILED error code for non-weekly-
hours failures instead of misleading INVALID_WEEKLY_HOURS.

Changes:
- Add SCHEDULE_GENERATION_FAILED to SCHEDULE_FETCH_ERROR_CODE
- Catch errors from distributeTasksToSessions and rethrow as ScheduleFetchError
- Update actions test mock to include new error code

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden formatters against edge cases

Fix formatMinutes to treat only null/undefined/NaN/Infinity/negative as
missing (0 now correctly returns '0 min'). Fix formatWeeklyHours to round
to 2 decimal places avoiding floating-point noise. Replace ad-hoc switch
statements in formatSkillLevel and formatLearningStyle with Record lookups.

Changes:
- Guard negative minutes with minutes < 0 check
- Round hours via parseFloat(hours.toFixed(2)) before formatting
- Replace switch/case with SKILL_LEVEL_LABELS and LEARNING_STYLE_LABELS maps
- Update tests for new 0/negative/NaN/float behaviors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): harden IP validation and implement true sliding window

Replace permissive IPv6 regex patterns with Node's net.isIP() for strict
IP validation, and convert the fixed-window rate limiter to a true
sliding window using per-request timestamps.

Changes:
- Replace IPV4_PATTERN, IPV6_PATTERN, IPV6_V4_PATTERN with net.isIP()
- Change WindowEntry from {count, windowStart} to {timestamps: number[]}
- Prune expired timestamps on each check, only record allowed requests
- Compute retryAfter from oldest active timestamp
- Update getRemainingRequests and getResetTime for timestamp-based logic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address PR review comments for APS DSL compliance

build-skill.md:
- Fix elicit-domain SET sources to use USER_INPUT for inference
- Initialize PLANNED_FILES as [] instead of empty string
- Move RETURN after SET PLAN_APPROVED to avoid unreachable code
- Accumulate validation results in FOREACH loop, return once after

aps-v1.1.16.agent.md + VS Code template:
- Replace non-DSL READ file at/CAPTURE from read result with
  USE `read/readFile`/CAPTURE from `read/readFile`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TTS v3: retry mechanism, Hmm fix, yay collapse, okee revert, duration tuning

- Added synthesis retry: up to 3 attempts, keeps longest audio
- Min duration: word_count * 0.15s (catches garbled short outputs)
- Changed Hm -> Hmm (fixes #24 producing numbers)
- Added yay+ collapse (Yayy -> yay)
- Reverted okee dokee experiment (worse than okey dokey)
- Round 32 scored 70% acceptable (new best)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: refactor OmnibusProvider to use omnibus-ts library (#17)

* feat: refactor OmnibusProvider to use @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts library

Replaces raw socket.io-client usage with the communicator() API from
omnibus-ts. This provides typed message handling with Zod validation,
automatic snake_case to camelCase conversion, and channel-based
message routing.

Changes:
- OmnibusProvider uses communicator() instead of io()
- Removed manual OmnibusPayload/OmnibusMessage types (now from library)
- Updated mock backend to use msgpack parser and channel-based events
- Removed debug console.log statements
- Connection status still uses allowExposeSocket until library supports
  events natively

Closes waterloo-rocketry/2025-software-issues#87

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use omnibus-ts connection API instead of allowExposeSocket

Replace raw socket event listeners with the new
connection.onConnectionChange() API from omnibus-ts. This removes
the need for allowExposeSocket: true just to monitor connection status.

- Remove allowExposeSocket flag from communicator options
- Replace socket.on('connect'/'disconnect'/'connect_error') with
  comm.connection.onConnectionChange()
- Import ConnectionStatus from @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts instead
  of local types
- Remove local ConnectionStatus type (now provided by omnibus-ts)

Depends on: waterloo-rocketry/omnibus-ts#6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump @waterloorocketry/omnibus-ts from 0.2.0 to 0.3.0

Bump omnibus-ts to v0.3.0 which includes the connection lifecycle API
(onConnectionChange, isConnected) already used by OmnibusProvider.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: average sensor samples and use message-level timestamp

Replace per-sample last-value logic with averaging all values in the
array, guard against empty arrays, and use the message-level timestamp
(baseTimestamp * 1000) instead of computing per-sample timestamps from
relativeTimestamps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

GitSplit: reviewable history edit scripts + safer Move-Commit rewrites

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

TTS cleaning v2: filler normalization, Bowzer/Gumba phonetics, short phrase padding, nocache bypass, period-comma conversion. Ralph loop R10-R24 avg 67pct acceptable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove lingering Notion-facing references from the product surface and
documentation now that the integration is no longer planned. This keeps
the UI, tests, and internal guidance aligned with the current Google
Calendar-only integration strategy while preserving the historical DB
migration files that still document the original removal.

  unsupported provider case

Co-authored-by: edvinklaebo <4660058+edvinklaebo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Edvin <edvin.klaebo@gmail.com>

Reduce and gate kinm trace volume (#15)

* Reduce and gate kinm trace volume

Remove the noisiest OTEL instrumentation from kinm and make the
remaining spans opt-in. This drops raw SQL and argument attributes,
removes redundant adapter and DB helper spans, and replaces
high-cardinality list and watch attributes with coarse flags and
counts.

Add KINM_TRACE_LEVEL with off, basic, and verbose levels and default
it to off. Spans in this package now only start when that level
allows them and the incoming context already carries a parent span,
which keeps standalone background usage quiet while preserving HTTP
request traces when enabled.

* Update pkg/db/strategy.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: kinm
repository.description: Kinm is not Mink

commit.message:
refactor: centralize test credential constants into testing::credentials (#829)

* refactor: centralize test credential constants into testing::credentials

Scattered test credential strings (API keys, OAuth tokens, crypto keys,
Telegram tokens, session tokens) across ~25 files made security auditing
harder and created unnecessary duplication. Centralize all test-only fake
credentials into a new `src/testing/credentials.rs` module with named
constants and a shared `test_secrets_store()` helper.

- Convert `src/testing.rs` to directory module (`src/testing/mod.rs`)
- Add `src/testing/credentials.rs` with ~30 named constants
- Replace hardcoded literals in 24 source files
- Deduplicate `test_store()` helper (was copy-pasted in 3 files)
- Leave leak_detector/shell/signature tests as-is (inline values
  aid readability for pattern detection tests)

[skip-regression-check]

commit.message:
Add monthly scheduling with set week [1st|2nd|3rd|4th|last]

Adds `set week [1st|2nd|3rd|4th|last]` to S'Up on the nth weekday of
every month (e.g. 2nd Monday). Use `unset week` to revert to the
weekly schedule. When monthly mode is active, `sup_every_n_weeks` is
ignored.

- Channel model: `sup_week_of_month` field (nil=weekly, 1–5), `WEEK_OF_MONTH_ORDINALS`
  lookup table, `monthly_sup_date` helper (handles "last" via end-of-month math),
  monthly-aware `sup?` and `next_sup_at`, display helpers `sup_week_of_month_s`
  and `sup_schedule_s`
- Commands: `set_week_of_month`, `unset_week_of_month`, wired into
  `channel_set`/`channel_unset`, `channel_info` uses `sup_schedule_s`
- API presenter: exposes `sup_week_of_month` and `sup_week_of_month_s`
- Help, changelog, and specs updated

Closes https://github.com/dblock/slack-sup2/issues/40

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: slack-sup2
repository.description: Slack bot that generates fresh triads of channel members to meet every week in an informal standup, or S'Up.

commit.message:
fix: update SubscribeButton test assertions for AbortSignal

The SubscribeButton component now passes an AbortSignal to fetch calls.
Test assertions used exact matching which broke when the signal property
was added. Use expect.objectContaining() to match the relevant properties
without being brittle to additional fetch options.

Changes:
- Use expect.objectContaining() in both fetch assertion tests
- Tests now pass regardless of additional fetch options like signal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct misleading "Redirecting…" text in PlanPendingState

The component calls router.refresh() when status is ready, which
re-fetches server component data in place without navigating. The UI
text said "Redirecting…" which is inaccurate since the URL never changes.

Changes:
- Replace "Redirecting…" with "Loading…" in PlanPendingState ready state
- Update secondary copy to "Your plan is ready. Preparing the view."

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): reduce API payload size (#14)

* fix(ci): reduce API payload size

Evaluate call (~44KB -> ~26KB):
- Remove README.md from codebase context (12KB of user docs adds
  no value for classification/planning)
- Halve the char cap from 60k to 20k chars (~5k tokens)

Implement call (~44KB -> ~3-8KB typically):
- Load only ev.plan.affected_files instead of the full codebase;
  the evaluation already identified which files need changes
- Falls back to all Lua files if affected_files is empty/unreadable
- Cap raised to 40k chars since this call needs richer context

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml

the file types that it will emit, is either lua, markdown, txt (vim doc, in doc folder), others are no consideration for now

commit.message:
Fix login flow, tunnel status, admin rename, consolidate portal

- fix: login server action called wrong endpoint (GET→POST /api/portal/login)
- fix: isRedirectError re-throw to prevent catch block swallowing Next.js redirects
- fix: tunnel status stuck offline — add UpdateTunnelOnline() called on agent poll
- fix: NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTROL_API_URL use relative /control path to avoid mixed content
- feat: admin can inline-rename tunnel hostnames (pencil icon in tunnels drawer)
- feat: new API route /api/admin/routes/[id] for hostname rename with uniqueness check
- refactor: /portal login page replaced with redirect to /login (single entry point)
- fix: /portal/dashboard redirects to /login (not /portal), handles tunnel_id query param
  from server action redirect to initialize localStorage credentials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix pylint issues to achieve 10.00/10 score

Fixed all pylint warnings:
- Replaced f-string logging with lazy % formatting in pr_comment.py
- Removed unused function parameters (repo_name, current_pr)
- Added pylint: disable=protected-access for test files
- Updated test to match new function signature

Changes:
- pr_comment.py: All logger calls now use lazy % formatting
- pr_comment.py: Removed unused repo_name parameter from _build_comment
- test_auth.py: Added protected-access disable comment
- test_pr_comment.py: Added protected-access disable, fixed test call

Test status: 190 tests passing, 99% coverage, 10.00/10 pylint

commit.message:
fix(init): add AWS credentials and fix health check in init-dynamodb.sh

- Add export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET_ACCESS_KEY/DEFAULT_REGION (was missing,
  causing silent failure of aws dynamodb create-table)
- Replace awslocal with aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4566
- Fix health check grep: accept both 'available' and 'running' status
- Verified: Bookings table now created successfully from clean slate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial status tracking on book creation

- Allow StatusUpdate.before to be NULL (before=null means 'added with
  this status', no prior state)
- BookCreate accepts status (default: to-read) and added_at (datetime,
  default: now) fields
- create_book route always writes an initial StatusUpdate(before=None,
  after=status, changed_at=added_at) so full history is recorded from
  the moment a book is added
- StatusUpdateRead and StatusUpdateExport: before is now Optional
- Add status select and date picker to the web add book form (Optional
  details section); default status is 'want to read', date defaults to
  today
- Alembic migration: nullable_statusupdate_before

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
